prs.title: [Bug] Fix GLM-5.1 running error on ROCm platform
prs.author: qli88
prs.body_head:
1. AITER MLA implementation requires num_heads>=16. Add padding when necessary for both regular MLA and sparse MLA;
2. An AITER bug will cause divided_by_zero error when num_heads is too small. Set it to num_heads to circumvent, and will remove it when corresponding AITER PR gets merged.

## Test Plan
__To verify sparse mla padding works correctly:__
1. **Padding added since MLA num_heads==8:**
VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER=1 vllm serve /data/GLM-5.1-FP8/ -tp 8 --trust-remote-code  --gpu-memory-utilization 0.8

2. **No padding since MLA num_heads==16:**
VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER=1 vllm serve /data/GLM-5.1-FP8/ -tp 4 --trust-remote-code  --gpu-memory-utilization 0.8

__To verify regular mla padding works correctly:__

3. **No padding since MLA num_heads==16:**

VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER=1 vllm serve /data/DeepSeek-R1/ -tp 8 --trust-remote-code  --gpu-memory-utilization 0.8

__Accuracy test command:__
lm_eval --model local-completions \
  --tasks gsm8k \
  --output_path ./results \
  --log_samples \
  --num_fewshot 20 \
  --model_args model=/data/GLM-5.1-FP8/,base_url=http://localhost:8000/v1/completions,num_concurrent=5,max_retries=3,timeout=3000,seed=1234

## Test Result

1. GLM 5.1, TP=8

|Tasks|Version|     Filter     |n-shot|  Metric   |   |Value |   |Stderr|
|-----|------:|----------------|-----:|-----------|---|-----:|---|-----:|
|gsm8k|      3|flexible-extract|    20|exact_match|↑  |0.9447|±  |0.0063|
|     |       |strict-match    |    20|exact_match|↑  |0.9447|±  |0.0063|

2. GLM 5.1, TP=4

prs.title: [NVFP4][Hopper/AMD Instinct] Add Triton kernels for NVFP4 dequantization and QDQ emulation
prs.author: fxmarty-amd
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

Add Triton kernels for NVFP4 dequantization and quantize-dequantize emulation to accelerate NVFP4 quantization emulation on devices that do not support this dtype natively (AMD Instinct MI3xx, H100), following https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/35733 and https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/35737.

The Triton kernels automatically replace the torch eager reference implementation when running on CUDA devices, with significant perf improvements, and avoids launching many independent kernel when using `--enforce-eager`, with back and forth HBM read/write.

These fused kernels  also help to stop hitting https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/180341 and https://github.com/ROCm/rocm-systems/issues/5071 where many stacked kernels calls from `dequantize_to_dtype` and `ref_nvfp4_quant_dequant` result in HSA crashes in recent ROCm versions / pytorch nightly (`vllm bench serve` with NVFP4 emulation on MI355X is not runnable without `HSA_TOOLS_DISABLE_REGISTER=1` due to these crashes - adding this env var is very slow).


This PR is submitted independently from https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/35737, but closely related to it.

## Test Plan

```python
pytest tests/models/quantization/test_nvfp4.py::test_triton_dequantize_nvfp4 -vvvvv -s 
pytest tests/models/quantization/test_nvfp4.py::test_triton_nvfp4_quant_dequant -vvvvv -s
```

## Test Result

The tests verify:
1. Correctness: Triton kernels produce bitwise-identical results to CPU reference (atol=0, rtol=0)
2. Coverage: Tests multiple tensor shapes (2D/3D), sizes, and global scale values
3. Real weights: Dequantization test uses actual NVFP4 weights from `nvidia/Qwen3-30B-A3B-NVFP4` checkpoint
4. Performance: Benchmarks show speedup vs. CPU reference implementation

## `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=7 pytest tests/models/quantization/test_nvfp4.py -s -vvvvv -k "test_triton_dequantize_nvfp4"`

```
  dequantize 2D base [512, 1024]:
    triton:    median=0.007ms, min=0.0

prs.title: [Core][WIP] Check for GPU<->CPU sync during CI
prs.author: njhill
prs.body_head:
vLLM now uses asynchronous scheduling by default and in the majority of cases. Performance relies on the absence of any gpu<->cpu synchronizations on the main cuda stream, but such syncs can be opaque and it is easy for them to creep in accidentally.

This change adds a `VLLM_GPU_SYNC_CHECK` env var which enables `torch.cuda.set_sync_debug_mode` for the model forward pass and sampler, so that we can easily check for such syncs.

I'm trying first to enable it globally in the CI to flush out syncs that need to be fixed or where they are unavoidable and the check needs to be suppressed. Will then probably split the fixes into separate PR(s).
